Defination: A detailed bill from the seller to the buyer, listing goods, value, and payment terms.
Purpose: Used by customs to assess duties and taxes
If not available, whats the issue?: Shipment is blocked at customs; fines for under-declaration; delays in seller payment.

Defination: An itemized list of products, including weights, dimensions, and quantities.
Purpose: Determines tariff treatment and eligibility for duty benefits under trade agreements.
If not available, whats the issue?: Cargo could be blocked by customs; detention, and demurrages will be applied

Defination: Document certifying the country in which the goods were manufactured or produced.
Purpose: Eligibility for Duty Free Concessions / FTA Benefits
If not available, whats the issue?: Cargo does not enter the destination country, or higher trade import duties; trade benefit denied

Defination: Statutory declaration of export cargo details/value.
Purpose: Government Export Permission
If not available, whats the issue?: Goods cannot leave the country

Defination: Confirmation from shipping line or forwarder that space has been allocated on a specific vessel for the cargo.
Purpose: Space Reservation on Vessel
If not available, whats the issue?: Shipment delayed

Defination: A legal contract between shipper and carrier acknowledging receipt of cargo for shipment. Acts as a title document.
Purpose: Title of Goods; Proof of Shipment
If not available, whats the issue?: Cargo cannot be released at deatination

Defination: A bank guarantee issued by the importer's bank assuring payment to the exporter upon meeting stated conditions.

If not available, whats the issue?: Payment Risk / Fraud

Defination: Document evidencing that cargo is insured against loss or damage during transit.

If not available, whats the issue?: No claim possible if cargo is damaged/ lost

If not available, whats the issue?: Shipment refused or fumigated at cost at destination

Who recevies it (If Any): Shipping Line, Port Authority, Customs Authorities

If not available, whats the issue?: Shipment refused; legal liability for exporter

Defination: Issued by government authority confirming goods (agri, food) are free from pests and meet importing country's standards.

If not available, whats the issue?: Consignment rejected or destroyed at port

If not available, whats the issue?: Shipment delayed

If not available, whats the issue?: Purchase delays

Defination: Confirms product quality/specifications through test results.

Defination: The main contract of carriage issued by the airline.

Defination: A document issued by a forwarder for a specific shipment.

Defination: A document certifying the country where goods were made.

Defination: The trade association for the world's airlines.

Defination: A pricing technique using volume instead of actual weight.

Defination: A formal statement of goods entering a country.

Defination: A list of all cargo on a specific flight.

Defination: A document signed by the receiver confirming arrival.

Defination: The projected time the aircraft will leave.

Defination: A status given to shippers who have passed security audits.

Defination: A document confirming space is reserved on a flight.

Defination: A secured facility where goods are stored before duties are paid.

Defination: A standard unit of measurement for cargo volume.

Defination: Short-distance trucking (e.g., from airport to warehouse).

Defination: Proof that cargo has been scanned for explosives/threats.

Defination: A freight quote that includes all surcharges (fuel, security).

Defination: An extra fee to cover fluctuating jet fuel prices.

Defination: Document certifying plants/food are free from pests.

Defination: A document detailing chemical properties and risks.

Defination: A combined document for value and content details.

Defination: Running a commercial vehicle or container without any cargo (empty).
Purpose: To relocate equipment to a high- demand area where cargo is waiting.
If not available, whats the issue?: High fuel waste and loss of revenue for the transport department.

Defination: A shipping mode for relatively small freight that doesn't fill an entire truck.
Purpose: To save costs by sharing truck space with other shippers.
If not available, whats the issue?: High shipping costs if forced to pay for a full truck (FTL) for small loads.

Defination: An order for a product that is temporarily out of stock but expected soon.
Purpose: To retain a sale and notify the customer that the item will ship later.
If not available, whats the issue?: Lost sales; customer dissatisfaction; lack of demand forecasting.

Defination: A numbered authorization used to return a product to the warehouse.
Purpose: To track and validate returns for inspection, repair, or refund.
If not available, whats the issue?: Chaos in the returns area; unable to verify if a return is legitimate.

Defination: A quality check performed on finished pallets right before they are loaded.
Purpose: To ensure the right items, labels, and packaging are present before leaving.
If not available, whats the issue?: High "cost of quality" due to shipping errors and customer complaints.

Defination: A document showing how long stock has been sitting in the warehouse.
Purpose: To identify slow-moving orobsolete stock that needs to be cleared.
If not available, whats the issue?: High holding costs; capital tied up in "dead" inventory.

Defination: A designated space for goods that are damaged, expired, or pending inspection.
Purpose: To prevent "unfit" stock from being mixed with sellable inventory.
If not available, whats the issue?: Risk of shipping damaged or unsafe goods to customers.

Defination: An outsourced provider that handles warehouse and distribution services.
Purpose: To allow a company to scale operations without owning physical warehouses.
If not available, whats the issue?: High overhead costs for small businesses trying to manage their own logistics.

Defination: An inventory management method where the oldest stock is shipped first.
Purpose: To ensure product rotation, especially for perishable items or electronics.
If not available, whats the issue?: Product spoilage; obsolete stock; financial losses due to expired goods.

Defination: Organizing the warehouse based on how frequently items are picked.
Purpose: To place "fast-movers" near the shipping area to reduce travel time.
If not available, whats the issue?: High labor costs; slow fulfillment cycles; worker fatigue.

Defination: A diagram showing how cases should be stacked on a pallet for stability.
Purpose: To maximize space and prevent products from collapsing during transit.
If not available, whats the issue?: Damaged goods due to shifting; inefficient use of truck space.

Defination: A system using lights and LEDs on racks to guide pickers to items.
Purpose: To increase picking speed and accuracy by removing the need\ for paper.
If not available, whats the issue?: High error rates; slower training time for new warehouse staff.

Defination: A contract defining the expected level of service (e.g., shipping speed).
Purpose: To set measurable performance standards and penalties for failure.
If not available, whats the issue?: Lack of accountability; inconsistent service quality; disputes.

Defination: A handheld device used to scan barcodes and update the WMS in real-time.
Purpose: To eliminate manual data entry and ensure inventory accuracy.
If not available, whats the issue?: High human error; delayed data updates; reliance on paper logs.

Defination: A preliminary bill of sale sent to buyers in advance of a shipment.
Purpose: To provide a formal quote for customs or to secure pre-payment.
If not available, whats the issue?: Delays in financing; customs clearance issues for international trade.

Defination: An inventory method where the most recently produced items are sold first.
Purpose: Used for non-perishable goods or for specific tax/accounting benefits.
If not available, whats the issue?: Outdated stock stays at the back; potential for inventory "drift."

Defination: A pricing technique using volume (L x W x H) rather than actual weight.
Purpose: To charge for the space a package occupies on a truck or plane.
If not available, whats the issue?: Unexpectedly high shipping costs for light but bulky items.

Defination: The process of taking individual items and bundling them into a single unit.
Purpose: To simplify the sale of "sets" or "bundles" and speed up shipping.
If not available, whats the issue?: Inefficient picking; inability to sell promotional bundles quickly.

Defination: A picking strategy where multiple orders are grouped and picked together.
Purpose: To reduce travel time by picking for many orders in one "wave" or trip.
If not available, whats the issue?: Extreme congestion in aisles; sloworder processing for high volumes.

Defination: A report documenting the difference between recorded and actual stock.
Purpose: To identify loss due to theft, damage, or administrative errors.
If not available, whats the issue?: Financial leakage; inaccurate stock levels leading to overselling.

Defination: Automated Storage and Retrieval Systems; computer-controlled systems for placing/retrieving loads.
Purpose: To automate high-density storage and reduce manual labor.
If not available, whats the issue?: High labor costs; slow retrieval in massive facilities.

Defination: An intermediate floor built between main floors to increase storage space.
Purpose: To double or triple usable floor space without expanding the building footprint.
If not available, whats the issue?: Overcrowding; wasted vertical space; high real estate costs.

Defination: Padding material (air bags, foam, scrap) used to protect goods during transport.
Purpose: To prevent movement and damage of goods inside a container or box.
If not available, whats the issue?: High rate of damaged goods/returns; customer dissatisfaction.

Defination: A process where the receiver counts stock without knowing the expected quantity.
Purpose: To ensure the clerk actually counts the stock rather than "rubber- stamping" the PO.
If not available, whats the issue?: Inaccurate inventory counts; hidden shipping discrepancies.

Defination: A document issued by a carrier giving details and instructions relating to the shipment.
Purpose: To act as a non-negotiable receipt and tracking document for air/rail freight.
If not available, whats the issue?: Inability to track cargo; legal issues at transit checkpoints.

Defination: Software that tracks and evaluates the productivity of warehouse workers.
Purpose: To optimize staffing levels and identify training needs or high performers.
If not available, whats the issue?: Low productivity; inability to track labor costs per order.

Defination: A formal document recording accidents, near-misses, or equipment damage.
Purpose: To track safety trends and implement corrective actions.
If not available, whats the issue?: Repeated accidents; legal liability; high insurance premiums.

Defination: A specific area where goods can be landed, handled, and manufactured without customs intervention.
Purpose: To encourage commerce by deferring or eliminating duties and taxes.
If not available, whats the issue?: Competitive disadvantage; high upfront customs costs.

Defination: A method where pickers are assigned to specific "zones" and pick only items in that area.
Purpose: To minimize travel time by keeping workers in a confined section of the warehouse.
If not available, whats the issue?: Inefficient travel paths; "bottlenecks" if one zone is busier than others.

Defination: Equipment used to bridge the gap between a warehouse dock and a truck bed.
Purpose: To allow safe and smooth loading/ unloading of heavy pallets.
If not available, whats the issue?: Forklift accidents; damaged goods during loading; slow operations.

Defination: The ability to track a group of items produced under the same conditions/date.
Purpose: To facilitate targeted recalls in case of a product defect.
If not available, whats the issue?: Inability to perform recalls; massive legal/ safety risks.

Defination: The computer-to-computer exchange of business documents in a standard format.
Purpose: To automate communication between suppliers, warehouses, and retailers.
If not available, whats the issue?: Slow manual data entry; high risk of clerical errors.

Defination: A system where workers wear headsets and receive verbal picking instructions.
Purpose: To allow "eyes-up, hands-free" picking for higher speed and safety.
If not available, whats the issue?: Slower picking; higher error rates due to distractions.

Defination: The process of moving items within a warehouse to optimize space or picking paths.
Purpose: To adjust storage based on changing demand (e.g., seasonal items).
If not available, whats the issue?: Inefficient picking; "dead" space in high- traffic areas.

Defination: Mechanical equipment used for the movement and storage of goods (e.g., forklifts).
Purpose: To move heavy loads that cannot be handled manually.
If not available, whats the issue?: Inability to move stock; high risk of worker injury.

Defination: A log showing which employees are trained on different warehouse tasks.
Purpose: To ensure operational flexibility when staff members are absent.
If not available, whats the issue?: Operational halts if a "key" person is missing; low morale.

Defination: Inventory that has no sales or usage for a long period of time.
Purpose: To identify items that should be liquidated or disposed of to save space.
If not available, whats the issue?: High holding costs; lack of space for new, profitable items.

Defination: A storage system using inclined rollers to allow boxes to slide forward.
Purpose: To ensure FIFO at the picking face and reduce reaching for workers.
If not available, whats the issue?: Inefficient picking; messy aisles; stock hidden at the back.

Defination: A diagram or list showing how cargo should be arranged in a truck/container.
Purpose: To balance weight distribution and ensure the last-stop items are loaded last.
If not available, whats the issue?: Vehicle instability/accidents; excessive unloading time at stops.

Defination: A record of temperature readings for perishable goods during storage/transit.
Purpose: To prove that food or medicine remained at safe temperatures.
If not available, whats the issue?: Product spoilage; health risks; rejection of entire shipments.

Defination: A thin, pallet-sized sheet of plastic or fiberboard used for heavy loads.
Purpose: To save space and weight in shipping containers compared to wooden pallets.
If not available, whats the issue?: Inefficient space usage; higher shipping costs due to pallet weight.

Defination: A short, sturdy post installed to protect racks and walls from forklift impact.
Purpose: To prevent structural damage to the warehouse during vehicle maneuvers.
If not available, whats the issue?: Expensive repairs; risk of rack collapse from accidental bumps.

Defination: A situation where storage cells are only partially filled, wasting space.
Purpose: Term used to identify and fix inefficient storage patterns.
If not available, whats the issue?: Wasted storage capacity; increased footprint costs.

Defination: Extra tasks like labeling, gift wrapping, or assembly performed in the warehouse.
Purpose: To customize products for the end consumer before they ship.
If not available, whats the issue?: Missed revenue; inability to meet specific retailer requirements.

Defination: The final leg of the logistics journey, from the warehouse to the customer's door.
Purpose: To ensure the product reaches the final user efficiently.
If not available, whats the issue?: Poor customer experience; high shipping costs; delivery failures.

Defination: Stacking goods directly on the floor of a container rather than on pallets.
Purpose: To maximize every cubic inch of space in an ocean container.
If not available, whats the issue?: Slower unloading times; requires more manual labor at the destination.

Defination: Picking all the items for a group of orders at once to minimize trips.
Purpose: To increase efficiency by visiting one location to fulfill multiple orders.
If not available, whats the issue?: Extremely high travel time for pickers; low productivity.

Defination: Storage racks with arms that extend out, used for long items like pipes or lumber.
Purpose: To safely store oversized or awkwardly shaped materials.
If not available, whats the issue?: Safety hazards; damage to long materials; inefficient storage.

Defination: A type of forklift designed to reach into deep racking systems.
Purpose: To operate in narrow aisles and reach high-density storage levels.
If not available, whats the issue?: Inability to use high-level storage; requirement for wider aisles.

Defination: A safety device that hooks onto a truck's rear underride guard to prevent it from moving.
Purpose: To prevent "trailer creep" or accidental pull-outs while loading.
If not available, whats the issue?: Forklift falls from the dock; serious injuries or death.

Defination: A large, pallet-sized corrugated box used for bulk shipping or scrap collection.
Purpose: To consolidate loose parts or bulk waste for easy transport.
If not available, whats the issue?: Messy warehouse floor; difficulty handling bulk small items.

Defination: A tag that uses radio waves to transmit data about an item without direct line-of-sight.
Purpose: To allow for instant, bulk counting of items in a box or pallet.
If not available, whats the issue?: Manual scanning required; slower receiving and inventory counts.

Defination: Grouping items from different areas of the warehouse into one single shipment.
Purpose: To reduce shipping costs and provide a better "unboxing" experience.
If not available, whats the issue?: High shipping costs (paying for multiple boxes for one order).

Defination: The total time it takes to complete a warehouse process (e.g., dock-to-stock).
Purpose: To measure and improve the speed of warehouse operations.
If not available, whats the issue?: Process bottlenecks; inability to meet customer delivery promises.

Defination: The fluctuation of inventory levels based on seasonal demand.
Purpose: To plan for temporary labor and extra space during peak seasons.
If not available, whats the issue?: Overstaffing during slow

Defination: An internal document requesting the purchase of goods or services.
Purpose: To initiate the purchasing process and secure budget approval.
If not available, whats the issue?: Unauthorized spending; procurement cannot officially begin sourcing.

Defination: A document sent to suppliers asking for pricing and terms for specific items.
Purpose: To compare prices and select the most cost-effective vendor.
If not available, whats the issue?: Overpaying for goods; inability to compare market rates fairly.

Defination: A detailed document seeking comprehensive solutions and pricing for complex projects.
Purpose: To evaluate potential suppliers based on value methodology and price.
If not available, whats the issue?: Selection of an unqualified vendor; misalignment on project scope and deliverables.

Defination: A preliminary document used to gather general information about potential suppliers.
Purpose: To build a database of potential vendors for future sourcing.
If not available, whats the issue?: Lack of market awareness; missing out on capable new suppliers.

Defination: An internal document acknowledging the delivery and acceptance of goods.
Purpose: To confirm items were received in good condition before payment.
If not available, whats the issue?: Paying for goods that were never delivered or arrived damaged.

Defination: The end-to-end process cycle from identifying a need to paying the supplier.
Purpose: To streamline purchasing and ensure accurate financial settlement.
If not available, whats the issue?: Process bottlenecks; delayed vendor payments; financial inaccuracies.

Defination: The process of comparing the Purchase Order, the GRN, and the supplier invoice.
Purpose: To ensure payment is only made for goods requested and received.
If not available, whats the issue?: Invoice fraud; overpayment; paying for unreceived goods.

Defination: A tracking tool used to evaluate a vendor's performance over time.
Purpose: To measure KPIs like delivery time, quality, and responsiveness.
If not available, whats the issue?: Continuing to use poor-performing vendors; supply chain disruptions.

Defination: A comprehensive list of raw materials, components, and instructions required to construct a product.
Purpose: To ensure exactly the right materials are sourced for production.
If not available, whats the issue?: Purchasing incorrect parts; complete halt of the manufacturing line.

Defination: A financial estimate combining the purchase price and the direct/indirect costs of operation.
Purpose: To evaluate the long-term cost of an asset beyond just the initial price tag.
If not available, whats the issue?: Buying a "cheap" item that ends up costing excessively in maintenance and operation.

Defination: An arrangement where the supplier manages and replenishes inventory at the buyer's location.
Purpose: To reduce inventory holding costs and prevent stockouts.
If not available, whats the issue?: Higher warehousing costs; frequent stockouts of critical materials.

Defination: A long-term agreement with a supplier to buy goods at a set price over a period of time.
Purpose: To secure volume discounts and simplify repetitive purchasing.
If not available, whats the issue?: Constant administrative work for minor orders; lost volume discounts.

Defination: The process of collecting, cleansing, and evaluating purchasing data.
Purpose: To identify cost-saving opportunities and understand spending patterns.
If not available, whats the issue?: Unnoticed rogue spending; missed opportunities for bulk negotiations.

Defination: The total time elapsed between placing an order and receiving the goods.
Purpose: To accurately plan inventory levels and production schedules.
If not available, whats the issue?: Stockouts; production delays while waiting for materials to arrive.

Defination: The process of identifying, evaluating, and engaging suppliers.
Purpose: To find the most reliable and cost-effective supply base.
If not available, whats the issue?: Limited supplier options leading to higher costs and lower quality.

Defination: The business-to-business requisitioning and purchasing of goods over the internet.
Purpose: To automate manual procurement tasks and increase transparency.
If not available, whats the issue?: Slow manual processes; high paperwork costs; human errors in ordering.

Defination: A contract negotiated to govern all future transactions and agreements between parties.
Purpose: To speed up future contracts by agreeing on foundational terms upfront.
If not available, whats the issue?: Lengthy legal negotiations for every single minor purchase or project.

Defination: A legal contract protecting sensitive information shared during the sourcing process.
Purpose: To ensure proprietary company data is not shared with competitors.
If not available, whats the issue?: Data breaches; intellectual property theft.

Defination: A systematic approach to evaluating and managing vendor relationships.
Purpose: To build strategic partnerships and drive continuous improvement.
If not available, whats the issue?: Strained vendor relations; lack of innovation or priority during shortages.

Defination: Purchasing goods or services outside of established contracts or procedures.
Purpose: N/A (This is a negative behavior tracked to prevent it)
If not available, whats the issue?: Loss of negotiated discounts; compliance risks; budget overruns.

Defination: The proactive management of a contract from initiation to renewal or termination.
Purpose: To ensure compliance and avoid missing renewal deadlines.
If not available, whats the issue?: Expired contracts; unintentional auto-renewals; compliance violations.

Defination: The process of collecting data and integrating a new vendor into the purchasing system.
Purpose: To ensure the vendor meets all legal and financial compliance standards before doing business.
If not available, whats the issue?: Fraudulent vendors; tax compliance issues; payment delays.

Defination: A comprehensive approach to sourcing that focuses on the total value and long-term goals.
Purpose: To align purchasing strategies with the overall business objectives.
If not available, whats the issue?: Tactical buying that focuses only on lowest price rather than overall value.

Defination: A strategy where materials are ordered to arrive exactly when they are needed in production.
Purpose: To minimize inventory holding costs and reduce warehouse space requirements.
If not available, whats the issue?: High storage costs; inventory obsolescence; capital tied up in stock.

Defination: The smallest amount of stock a supplier is willing to sell in a single order.
Purpose: To ensure the supplier covers their setup and production costs.
If not available, whats the issue?: Orders are rejected by the supplier; forced to buy more stock than needed.

Defination: Extra inventory held to guard against unexpected spikes in demand or supply delays.
Purpose: To act as a buffer against supply chain disruptions.
If not available, whats the issue?: Severe stockouts leading to halted production or lost sales.

Defination: The practice of grouping similar items to buy them more efficiently.
Purpose: To leverage buying power and develop deep market expertise in specific areas.
If not available, whats the issue?: Fragmented purchasing; missed volume discounts across different departments.

Defination: A document defining project-specific activities, deliverables, and timelines.
Purpose: To set clear boundaries and expectations for a service contract.
If not available, whats the issue?: Scope creep; disputes over what work was actually paid for.

Defination: Verifying that the Purchase Order and the Invoice match (typically used for services where there is no GRN).
Purpose: To authorize payment for intangible services.
If not available, whats the issue?: Paying for services that were not agreed upon or authorized.

Defination: Actions taken to prevent future cost increases rather than reducing current costs.
Purpose: To protect the budget from inflation or upcoming price hikes.
If not available, whats the issue?: Uncontrolled budget inflation; hidden supply chain costs.

Defination: Tangible reductions in the unit price of goods or services compared to historical prices.
Purpose: To directly improve the company's bottom line profitability.
If not available, whats the issue?: Stagnant profitability; failure to leverage market changes.

Defination: The steps taken to handle a supplier invoice from receipt to final payment.
Purpose: To ensure timely and accurate settlement of debts.
If not available, whats the issue?: Late payment fees; damaged supplier relationships.

Defination: The conditions under which a seller will complete a sale (e.g. Net 30 or Net 60).
Purpose: To manage the company's cash flow effectively.
If not available, whats the issue?: Cash flow shortages; disputes over when payment is due.

Defination: A trade arrangement where goods are shipped and delivered before payment is due.
Purpose: To provide flexible purchasing without immediate upfront cash.
If not available, whats the issue?: High risk for the supplier; requires strong buyer creditworthiness.

Defination: International rules defining the responsibilities of buyers and sellers in global trade.
Purpose: To clearly define who pays for shipping and who bears the risk of loss.
If not available, whats the issue?: Legal disputes over damaged cargo; unexpected freight or insurance costs.

Defination: An event where multiple sellers compete to obtain business by lowering their prices.
Purpose: To rapidly drive down prices for highly standardized commodities.
If not available, whats the issue?: Overpaying for standard goods; slow manual negotiation processes.

Defination: Guidelines detailing the ethical and environmental standards expected from vendors.
Purpose: To ensure suppliers align with the company's ESG and legal standards.
If not available, whats the issue?: Public relations disasters; association with unethical labor or environmental practices.

Defination: Purchasing raw materials and components directly used in manufacturing the final product.
Purpose: To maintain the core production line and revenue generation.
If not available, whats the issue?: Production line stoppages; immediate loss of sales.

Defination: Purchasing goods and services needed for day-to-day operations (e.g. software or office supplies).
Purpose: To keep the business running and support internal employees.
If not available, whats the issue?: Inefficient workplace operations; employee frustration; unchecked operational spending.

Defination: A supplier's formal confirmation that they have received and accepted a Purchase Order.
Purpose: To confirm that pricing and delivery dates are agreed upon.
If not available, whats the issue?: Uncertainty if goods will arrive; supplier unexpectedly rejects the order later.

Defination: A percentage deduction offered by a seller if the buyer pays the invoice before its due date.
Purpose: To incentivize fast payment and improve the supplier's cash flow.
If not available, whats the issue?: Missed opportunities for immediate cost savings; higher overall purchase costs.

Defination: A document that formally authorizes the existence of a project.
Purpose: Provides the PM authority to use organizational resources.
If not available, whats the issue?: No clear authority; project may be seen as "unauthorized."

Defination: A justification for the project based on cost, benefits, and risks.
Purpose: To prove the project is worth the investment.
If not available, whats the issue?: Money may be wasted on projects with no ROI.

Defination: A list identifying all people or groups interested in the project.
Purpose: To track influence and communication needs.
If not available, whats the issue?: Key people might be ignored, leading to resistance.

Defination: A detailed description of project deliverables and boundaries.
Purpose: To define what is (and isn't) included in the project.
If not available, whats the issue?: "Scope creep" where work grows uncontrollably.

Defination: A hierarchical decomposition of the total scope of work.
Purpose: To break down complex tasks into manageable chunks.
If not available, whats the issue?: Tasks are missed; poor cost and time estimation.

Defination: The master document defining how the project is executed and controlled.
Purpose: To provide a baseline for progress and decision-making.
If not available, whats the issue?: Lack of direction; team works in silos.

Defination: A visual timeline showing tasks, durations, and dependencies.
Purpose: To track the schedule and see task overlaps.
If not available, whats the issue?: Deadlines are missed; team doesn't know what's next.

Defination: A log of potential "what-if" scenarios and their response plans.
Purpose: To identify and mitigate threats before they happen.
If not available, whats the issue?: Project is blindsided by predictable problems.

Defination: A chart showing who is Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, and Informed.
Purpose: To clarify roles and avoid confusion over task ownership.
If not available, whats the issue?: Duplicate work or tasks "falling through the cracks."

Defination: A formal proposal to modify any document, deliverable, or baseline.
Purpose: To ensure changes are evaluated for impact on cost/time.
If not available, whats the issue?: Uncontrolled changes lead to budget and time overruns.

Defination: A record of current problems affecting the project.
Purpose: To document, track, and resolve active hurdles.
If not available, whats the issue?: Problems are forgotten and escalate into disasters.

Defination: A regular update on the current health of the project.
Purpose: To keep stakeholders informed on progress vs. plan.
If not available, whats the issue?: Stakeholders lose trust or lack visibility.

Defination: Standards and criteria the project must meet to be successful.
Purpose: To ensure the final product is fit for use.
If not available, whats the issue?: High defect rates and client dissatisfaction.

Defination: A schedule showing when specific resources (people/equip) are available.
Purpose: To avoid over-allocating people or tools.
If not available, whats the issue?: Resource conflicts; team members are overworked.

Defination: A description of work to be done by an outside vendor.
Purpose: To clearly define what a contractor must deliver.
If not available, whats the issue?: Disputes with vendors over what was "promised."

Defination: A list of significant points or events in a project.
Purpose: To show high-level progress without getting lost in details.
If not available, whats the issue?: Hard to judge if the project is "on track" overall.

Defination: A strategy for who gets what info, when, and how.
Purpose: To ensure the right people are always informed.
If not available, whats the issue?: Information silos; stakeholders feel left out.

Defination: The sequence of tasks that determines the shortest project duration.
Purpose: To identify tasks that cannot be delayed without delaying the project.
If not available, whats the issue?: Inability to prioritize tasks that impact the end date.

Defination: A document recording what went well and what didn't.
Purpose: To improve performance in future projects.
If not available, whats the issue?: Repeating the same mistakes in the next project.

Defination: A formal document confirming the project is finished.
Purpose: To handover deliverables and release resources.
If not available, whats the issue?: Projects "drag on" indefinitely without a clean end.

Defination: An ordered list of everything that might be needed in the product.
Purpose: To serve as the single source of requirements for any changes.
If not available, whats the issue?: Team lacks a clear priority of what to build next.

Defination: A set of backlog items selected for a specific Sprint (short work cycle).
Purpose: To define the work the team commits to doing in the next 2-4 weeks.
If not available, whats the issue?: Lack of focus during the work cycle; over-commitment.

Defination: A graphic representation of work left to do versus time.
Purpose: To track the progress of a sprint and predict if work will finish.
If not available, whats the issue?: Team won't realize they are behind until the deadline hits.

Defination: A measure of the amount of work a team can tackle during a single period.
Purpose: To help in planning future capacity and timelines.
If not available, whats the issue?: Unrealistic scheduling and "guess-work" planning.

Defination: A measure of the financial effectiveness of a project ($EV / AC$).
Purpose: To see if the project is under or over budget.
If not available, whats the issue?: Financial "bleeding" goes unnoticed until funds run out.

Defination: A measure of schedule efficiency ($EV / PV$).
Purpose: To see if the project is ahead of or behind schedule.
If not available, whats the issue?: Delays are not quantified, making recovery difficult.

Defination: A record of all factors believed to be true without proof.
Purpose: To track variables that could become risks if proven false.
If not available, whats the issue?: Decisions are made based on "guesses" that may fail.

Defination: Specifically defined attributes to be measured (e.g., failure rate).
Purpose: To provide an objective way to measure "success."
If not available, whats the issue?: Quality is subjective; arguments over "finished" work.

Defination: A grid showing project resources assigned to each work package.
Purpose: To ensure every task has a body assigned to it.
If not available, whats the issue?: Resources are under-utilized or double-booked.

Defination: The practice of comparing planned results with actual results.
Purpose: To identify why the project is deviating from the plan.
If not available, whats the issue?: No understanding of why the project is failing or succeeding.

Defination: A document outlining the first formal meeting with all stakeholders.
Purpose: To align everyone on goals and set the tone for the project.
If not available, whats the issue?: Confusion from day one; lack of team cohesion.

Defination: A contract between a service provider and a customer.
Purpose: To define the level of service (uptime, speed) expected.
If not available, whats the issue?: No legal recourse if the service provider performs poorly.

Defination: A shared understanding of what it means for work to be complete.
Purpose: To ensure quality and prevent "half-baked" deliverables.
If not available, whats the issue?: Tasks are marked "done" but still require bug fixes later.

Defination: The original approved version of the project schedule or budget.
Purpose: To serve as the "yardstick" for measuring performance.
If not available, whats the issue?: No way to know if you are over budget or late.

Defination: Informal, natural language descriptions of features from the end-user's view.
Purpose: To capture functional requirements in a way that provides value.
If not available, whats the issue?: Features are built that the customer doesn't actually want.

Defination: A bar chart showing the amount of time a resource is scheduled.
Purpose: To visually identify resource overloads or "burnout."
If not available, whats the issue?: Team members are overworked while others sit idle.

Defination: A grid mapping the likelihood and severity of risks.
Purpose: To prioritize which risks need immediate attention.
If not available, whats the issue?: Waste of time focusing on "unlikely" or "minor" risks.

Defination: A list of all changes submitted and their current status.
Purpose: To keep a history of how the project evolved from the original plan.
If not available, whats the issue?: No record of why the budget or scope increased.

Defination: A formal sign-off from the customer on the end product.
Purpose: To legally and professionally close the project and stop work.
If not available, whats the issue?: The client keeps asking for "one more thing" forever.

Defination: A tool to document Suppliers, Inputs, Process, Outputs, and Customers.
Purpose: To provide a high-level view of a process before detailing it.
If not available, whats the issue?: Misunderstanding of where the project boundaries start/end.

Defination: A visualization tool to categorize potential causes of a problem.
Purpose: To identify the root cause of an issue rather than just symptoms.
If not available, whats the issue?: Solving the wrong problem; issues keep recurring.

Defination: A computerized mathematical technique to account for risk in quantitative analysis.
Purpose: To predict the probability of completing a project on a specific date.
If not available, whats the issue?: Unrealistic confidence in a single "best-case" date.

Defination: The lowest level of the Work Breakdown Structure (WBS).
Purpose: To define specific tasks that can be estimated and managed.
If not available, whats the issue?: Work is too vague to assign or estimate accurately.

Defination: A visual workflow tool that uses cards to represent work items.
Purpose: To manage work-in-progress (WIP) and optimize flow.
If not available, whats the issue?: Work gets "stuck" in bottlenecks without being noticed.

Defination: Key areas that must go right for the project to be a success.
Purpose: To focus the team's energy on the most important variables.
If not available, whats the issue?: Efforts are wasted on "nice-to-have" features that don't matter.

Defination: Budget or time set aside for "known-unknowns" (identified risks).
Purpose: To handle risks that were predicted in the Risk Register.
If not available, whats the issue?: Project runs out of money as soon as a single risk occurs.

Defination: Budget set aside for "unknown-unknowns" (unforeseen events).
Purpose: To provide a safety net for totally unexpected disasters.
If not available, whats the issue?: Project is cancelled if a random, unpredicted crisis hits.

Defination: A bar graph where values are plotted in decreasing order of frequency.
Purpose: To follow the 80/20 rule (focus on the 20% of causes creating 80% of problems).
If not available, whats the issue?: Fixing minor issues while the biggest failures are ignored.

Defination: The value of work actually performed expressed in terms of the budget.
Purpose: To measure the "true" progress of a project vs. money spent.
If not available, whats the issue?: Thinking you are "under budget" just because you haven't spent much.

Defination: A high-level visual summary that maps out the vision of a product over time.
Purpose: To communicate the long-term strategy and direction.
If not available, whats the issue?: The team focuses on immediate tasks and loses the "big picture."

Defination: A narrative description of products, services, or results to be delivered.
Purpose: To define the technical and performance requirements of a contract.
If not available, whats the issue?: Legal disputes over what was supposed to be built.

Defination: A tracking of velocity over multiple sprints.
Purpose: To see if the team is becoming more or less efficient over time.
If not available, whats the issue?: Inability to forecast long-term release dates for the product.

Defination: Notes from a meeting where the team discusses how to improve.
Purpose: To implement process improvements immediately.
If not available, whats the issue?: The team continues to work in an inefficient or toxic way.

Defination: An assessment of the practicality of a proposed plan or method.
Purpose: To determine if a project is technically or financially possible.
If not available, whats the issue?: Investing millions into a project that is impossible to finish.

Defination: A technique in which start/finish dates are adjusted based on resource constraints.
Purpose: To ensure no one person is assigned 60 hours of work in a 40-hour week.
If not available, whats the issue?: High staff turnover and burnout.

Defination: A list of tasks that rely on other tasks or external factors.
Purpose: To ensure "Task B" doesn't start before "Task A" is finished.
If not available, whats the issue?: Team members sit idle waiting for others to finish work.

Defination: A plan defining when and how to move a problem to higher management.
Purpose: To ensure big problems get the attention they need quickly.
If not available, whats the issue?: Junior staff try to fix massive problems alone, wasting time.

Defination: A plan to ensure the project benefits are actually achieved after closing.
Purpose: To track if the project actually improved the business as intended.
If not available, whats the issue?: The project is "finished," but the business never sees the profit.

Defination: A set of conditions that must be met before a deliverable is accepted.
Purpose: To remove ambiguity regarding "quality" or "completion."
If not available, whats the issue?: Rejection of work by the client at the very last minute.

Defination: A lean tool used to document every step in a process from start to finish.
Purpose: To identify and eliminate non- value-added activities (waste).
If not available, whats the issue?: The project team remains inefficient without knowing where the lag is.

Defination: A forecast of project costs to be paid that includes the "when" and "how much."
Purpose: To ensure the organization has the cash flow ready for project bills.
If not available, whats the issue?: Project stops abruptly because there is no cash to pay vendors.

Defination: A sensitivity analysis tool showing which risks have the most impact.
Purpose: To visually prioritize the risks that could swing the budget the most.
If not available, whats the issue?: Focus is spread thin across all risks instead of the "big hitters."

Defination: A technique to categorize requirements: Must have, Should have, Could have, Won't have.
Purpose: To manage scope when time or budget is limited.
If not available, whats the issue?: Everything is treated as "urgent," leading to total project burnout.

Defination: The rate at which the remaining work is decreasing.
Purpose: To predict the exact day the current workload will hit zero.
If not available, whats the issue?: Management sets unrealistic launch dates based on hope, not data.

Defination: Adjustments to the start/end of dependent tasks (Lead accelerates, Lag delays).
Purpose: To fine-tune the schedule for realistic transitions between tasks.
If not available, whats the issue?: The schedule is too rigid or physically impossible to execute.

Defination: A contract type where the price is set and does not change.
Purpose: To shift the financial risk onto the seller/vendor.
If not available, whats the issue?: Cost overruns by the vendor become the buyer's financial problem.

Defination: A contract where the buyer pays for costs plus a fixed fee.
Purpose: To encourage vendors to take on high-risk, experimental work.
If not available, whats the issue?: No incentive for the vendor to control costs; budget may spiral.

Defination: A management control point where scope, cost, and schedule are integrated.
Purpose: To track performance at a level above individual work packages.
If not available, whats the issue?: Hard to pinpoint which specific department is overspending.

Defination: A real-time visual display of Key Performance Indicators (KPIs).
Purpose: To provide an "at-a-glance" health check of the project.
If not available, whats the issue?: Stakeholders have to dig through 50-page reports to find basic info.

Defination: A technique used for constructing a schedule model (Boxes and Arrows).
Purpose: To visualize how all tasks are logically connected.
If not available, whats the issue?: Logic errors in the schedule (e.g., building the roof before the walls).

Defination: Supporting documentation explaining how cost/time figures were calculated.
Purpose: To provide transparency and "defend" the budget to the board.
If not available, whats the issue?: The budget looks like "made-up numbers" and gets cut by management.

Defination: A structured review of the procurement process from start to finish.
Purpose: To identify successes and failures in vendor management.
If not available, whats the issue?: Legal loopholes or bad vendor behaviors are repeated in next project.

Defination: The software or tools used to gather and distribute information.
Purpose: To centralize all project data (e.g., Jira, MS Project, Asana).
If not available, whats the issue?: Information is lost in email threads and personal spreadsheets.

Defination: A formal procedure for sanctioning project work.
Purpose: To ensure work is done at the right time and in the right sequence.
If not available, whats the issue?: Team members start tasks too early, wasting resources.

Defination: The rules, roles, and processes that guide project activities.
Purpose: To ensure the project aligns with the company's strategic goals.
If not available, whats the issue?: The project is successful technically but fails the company's mission.

Defination: A document detailing how the "end product" moves to the "end user."
Purpose: To ensure the project isn't "dropped" on users without training.
If not available, whats the issue?: The product is built but nobody knows how to maintain or use it.

Defination: A legal agreement between two entities to work together on a project.
Purpose: To define the partnership before a full contract is signed.
If not available, whats the issue?: Partners fight over intellectual property or profit sharing later.

Defination: A grid that links requirements from their origin to the deliverables.
Purpose: To ensure that every single requirement is actually tested and met.
If not available, whats the issue?: Features are missed during development, failing the client's needs.

Defination: The practice of adding extra features not requested by the client.
Purpose: (Often an error) Trying to "wow" the client with unrequested work.
If not available, whats the issue?: Wasted budget and time on things the client didn't ask for.

Defination: A high-level visual of all active projects within an organization.
Purpose: To ensure the mix of projects balances risk and strategic reward.
If not available, whats the issue?: The company takes on too many high-risk projects at once.

Defination: A projection of the budget deficit or surplus at the end of the project.
Purpose: To warn stakeholders exactly how much the project will be over/under budget.
If not available, whats the issue?: Financial surprises at the very end of the project when it's too late.

Defination: An audit to ensure the organization is ready to support the new product.
Purpose: To confirm that support staff, manuals, and tools are in place for launch.
If not available, whats the issue?: The project "goes live" but the support team is unable to help users.

Defination: An evaluation conducted months after the project is closed.
Purpose: To measure if the project actually delivered the expected long-term value.
If not available, whats the issue?: No one knows if the project actually solved the original business problem.

Defination: A legal document stating the work is substantially complete and safe for use.
Purpose: To hand over the responsibility/ liability of the site or product to the owner.
If not available, whats the issue?: Ongoing legal liability for the project team even after they have left.
